title: Portik
excerpt: A Go CLI for inspecting, explaining, and safely managing port ownership across local development environments.
description: Portik is a developer-focused CLI for finding which process owns a port, explaining why a listener is stuck, and recovering local services without falling back to ad hoc shell commands.
problemSolved: Port conflicts, hidden listeners, and container mappings routinely slow down local development because the underlying process state is scattered across multiple system tools.
architectureHighlights: Cross-platform inspection flow built around ss, lsof, ps, and Docker-aware lookups
Interactive TUI mode for live monitoring, history, and safe actions on busy ports
Command surface for explain, kill, restart, trace, graph, and watch workflows
role: Designed and implemented the CLI surface, local inspection model, and troubleshooting workflow for repeated port-debugging tasks.
contributions: Built command flows for who, explain, kill, restart, history, and graph operations
Added Bubble Tea based TUI support for interactive inspection
Documented Linux and macOS platform behavior, permissions, and recovery paths

title: Arpkit
excerpt: A zero-daemon CLI for profiling machine topology, cache layout, NUMA boundaries, and performance posture.
description: Arpkit is a Go command-line profiler for machine architecture. It reports CPU topology, cache hierarchy, NUMA layout, isolation settings, power scaling, and posture signals needed for performance engineering.
problemSolved: Performance investigations often begin without a reliable machine profile, making it harder to reason about topology, cache sharing, NUMA effects, and isolation posture.
architectureHighlights: Single-binary design with no daemon and no external command execution
Topology and posture collection across cache, memory, power, NUMA, and PCIe affinity
Pretty, JSON, and DOT output modes for both terminal use and automation
role: Built the profiling model, output formats, and platform-specific collection logic for fast machine-level inspection.
contributions: Implemented topology and cache discovery with Linux-first depth and macOS fallback paths
Added posture scoring and concise machine summaries for performance work
Shipped JSON and DOT export modes for downstream tooling and visualization

title: EsPlain
excerpt: A query explainer CLI for Elasticsearch that turns raw query JSON into readable execution detail.
description: EsPlain is a Go-based query explainer for Elasticsearch. It parses raw queries and combines explain, validate, and profile output into a more readable view for debugging expensive search behavior.
problemSolved: Elasticsearch debugging is often slowed down by dense query JSON and verbose profile output that are powerful but not immediately readable during tuning or incident response.
architectureHighlights: Integrates with Elasticsearch explain, validate, and profile APIs
Transforms query internals into human-readable trees and hotspot summaries
Supports both terminal-first analysis and generated HTML report output
role: Created the CLI concept, search-diagnostics integration, and reporting layer around Elasticsearch query analysis.
contributions: Parsed raw query JSON into readable execution components
Wired explain and profile responses into a single troubleshooting flow
Added operator-friendly output for identifying costly clauses quickly

title: Ephor
excerpt: A clipboard security agent for macOS that warns before sensitive data is pasted into browsers.
description: Ephor is a Python security utility that monitors global paste events, matches clipboard contents against secret-detection rules, and alerts the user before credentials are pasted into a browser.
problemSolved: Secrets can be pasted into the wrong destination surprisingly easily, especially while switching between terminals, browser tabs, and password managers.
architectureHighlights: Event-driven clipboard monitoring using macOS paste hooks
Rule-based secret detection loaded from YAML pattern sets
Standalone packaging path for running the agent outside a Python environment
role: Implemented the local agent flow, rule loading, and notification experience for a lightweight security guardrail.
contributions: Wired global paste detection into a background monitoring loop
Added regex-driven secret matching and browser-aware warnings
Documented packaging, permissions, and local runtime requirements

title: Amazgo
excerpt: REST APIs for scraping Amazon product data and persisting the results to MongoDB.
description: Amazgo exposes HTTP endpoints that scrape Amazon pages with Colly, normalize product fields such as title, image, price, and reviews, and store the results with timestamps in MongoDB.
problemSolved: Product extraction often starts as one-off scripts, which makes repeated scraping, structured persistence, and local reproducibility harder than it needs to be.
architectureHighlights: REST endpoints for direct product ingestion and scrape-trigger workflows
Scraping pipeline built on Colly for extracting structured product fields
Containerized local setup with MongoDB-backed persistence
role: Built the API handlers, scraping flow, and local development setup for structured product collection.
contributions: Implemented scrape and product endpoints for collecting page data
Mapped extracted fields into a stored document model with timestamps
Added Docker-based bootstrapping for the service and database

title: REST APIs with Go and MySQL
excerpt: A small backend service covering registration, login, token validation, and user management flows.
description: This project implements a straightforward Go API for register, login, token validation, list, update, and delete operations backed by MySQL tables for users and login tokens.
problemSolved: Backend fundamentals are easier to understand when auth flows, token handling, and relational persistence are kept explicit instead of hidden behind a large framework.
architectureHighlights: Route handlers for registration, login, token validation, and user updates
Relational schema split between users and login token state
Minimal file layout meant to keep backend control flow readable
role: Implemented the API surface, handler logic, and MySQL schema expectations for a small auth-oriented backend service.
contributions: Built endpoints for register, login, validate, list, update, and delete flows
Defined the MySQL tables used for users and login tokens
Kept the code structure intentionally small for clarity and extension
